---
title: "Visual Load Likert Analysis"
output: html_notebook
---
```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#Install only if needed
#install.packages("rlang")
#install.packages("ggplot2")
#install.packages("tidyverse")
#install.packages("likert")
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(likert)
library(ggstats)
library(dplyr)
```
# Read CSV
```{r}
raw_data <- read.csv("../processed/AllVisualisations_cleaned.csv", 
                     header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Remove first two rows (metadata?) and reset rownames
data <- raw_data[-c(1,2), ]
rownames(data) <- NULL

head(data)
```

```{r}
data$Q1_1 <- as.numeric(data$Q1_1)
data$Q1_2 <- as.numeric(data$Q1_2)
data$Q1_3 <- as.numeric(data$Q1_3)
data$Q1_4 <- as.numeric(data$Q1_4)
data$Q1_5 <- as.numeric(data$Q1_5)
data$Q1_6 <- as.numeric(data$Q1_6)

rankings <- data %>%
  select(Q1_1, Q1_2, Q1_3, Q1_4, Q1_5, Q1_6) %>%
  rename(
    `Arrow`            = Q1_1,
    `Radar`            = Q1_2,
    `Lights`           = Q1_3,
    `Arrow and Radar`  = Q1_4,
    `Radar and Lights` = Q1_5,
    `Lights and Arrow` = Q1_6
  ) %>%
  mutate(across(everything(),
                ~ factor(.x, levels = 1:6,
                          labels = c("1st","2nd","3rd","4th","5th","6th"),
                          ordered = TRUE)))
```

```{r, fig.height=6, fig.width=9}
rank_plot <- gglikert(
  rankings,
  sort = "ascending",         # sorts rows so best-ranked vis appears at top
  sort_method = "mean",
  labels_size = 3,
  totals_include_center = FALSE
) +
  #scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", direction = -1) +  # green=1st, red=6th
  ggtitle("Visualisation Rankings (1st = Best, 6th = Worst)")

ggsave(
  filename = "../graphs/Visualisation_Rankings.pdf",
  plot = rank_plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  units = "in"
)
```
